title: Design optional LLM analysis over sanitized evidence only
description:
Context: LLM analysis is useful later, but it should be downstream of deterministic source checks and prompt-injection detection.
Scope: model prompt contracts, input constraints, schemas, confidence/uncertainty reporting.
Acceptance criteria:
- Define which reduced artifact fields an LLM may see for semantic review.
- Define outputs as structured findings with evidence references, uncertainty, and no autonomous trust decision.
- Include an escalation path for complex/high-risk commits and suspicious mailing-list context.
- Document that raw git/email content remains hostile data, not instructions.
Risk notes:
- Avoid opaque maliciousness scores; require explanations tied to deterministic evidence.

title: Parse a single mailing-list message into normalized facts
description:
Context: Mailing-list context begins with one safely parsed message, not direct LLM ingestion of raw email.
Scope: MIME/email parser, normalized message schema, prompt-injection/static-risk hints.
Acceptance criteria:
- Given raw RFC822 email or mbox entry, emit stable JSON with message-id, date, from, to/cc/list ids, subject, in-reply-to/references, plain-text body excerpt, patch/diff presence, and attachment metadata.
- Detect and flag prompt-injection language, executable-looking snippets, URLs/domains, and truncation.
- Preserve source references while treating all message content as hostile input.
- Include tests for multipart messages, patch emails, replies, malformed headers, and huge bodies.
Risk notes:
- Email bodies and headers are hostile content; reducers must bound and label content before model use.

title: Implement malicious-change and integrity heuristics
description:
Context: Detect suspicious commits, multi-commit behavior, and out-of-band tree/artifact injection risks.
Scope: Provenance anomaly checks, multi-commit pattern checks, object/tree/artifact verification hooks.
Acceptance criteria:
- Check authorship, committer, signing, timestamp, review-path, and ref/object anomalies.
- Flag suspicious split changes and setup/use separation across multiple commits.
- Verify clean checkout/object reachability and document generated-artifact/binary comparison hooks.
Risk notes:
- Avoid false certainty; outputs must distinguish fact, heuristic, and uncertainty.
notes: Iteration notes: Treat malicious-change detection as a pipeline: deterministic static rules first for trivial attacks such as new compiler/static-analysis warnings or removed tests, plus prompt-injection checks, then optional LLM analysis over sanitized evidence.

title: Find mailing-list messages likely related to commits
description:
Context: Correlation should use deterministic evidence before semantic/model analysis.
Scope: candidate search over public mailing-list archives or local indexed messages.
Acceptance criteria:
- For a commit or range artifact, find likely related messages using timestamp windows, subject/patch-id cues, changed kernel subsystem, and relevant list identifiers.
- Prefer Linux public archives such as lore.kernel.org for the initial implementation.
- Emit candidates with scored evidence fields rather than a single opaque confidence number.
- Include tests with local fixture messages before relying on network access.
Risk notes:
- Network-fetched archive content is hostile; sanitize and normalize before privileged analysis.

title: Add Linux kernel subsystem impact hints
description:
Context: Start turning kernel commit ranges into practical retest guidance.
Scope: Linux-specific mapping data and impact analyzer.
Acceptance criteria:
- Map touched paths to likely kernel subsystems and maintainers where practical.
- Flag Kconfig, driver, arch, syscall, filesystem, networking, scheduler, memory-management, and security-sensitive surfaces.
- Emit retest hints linked back to changed files and commits.
Dependencies:
- blocked-by: deterministic git range facts.
Risk notes:
- Keep kernel-specific heuristics isolated from the reusable git-repo core.
notes: Iteration notes: Keep logic generic for Linux and eventually other repositories. Use a public Raspberry Pi kernel tree as the first kernel exemplar. Target-system-specific config/profile analysis is future work.

title: Parse a single git commit into normalized source facts
description:
Context: First reusable unit of analysis is one commit, treated as hostile git metadata plus source diff data.
Scope: src/kernel_diffguard commit parser, normalized artifact schema, tests with synthetic git repos.
Acceptance criteria:
- Given repo path and commit SHA, emit stable JSON with SHA, parents, tree, author/committer names/emails/timestamps, subject/body, touched paths, diff stats, rename/copy metadata, and bounded source diff excerpts.
- Use explicit separators when invoking git so multiline commit bodies cannot corrupt parsing.
- Mark commit message, path names, and diff text as untrusted input in the artifact.
- Include tests for multiline messages, weird paths, renames, empty commits, and large diffs with truncation metadata.
Risk notes:
- Do not let commit messages, path names, or diff text become instructions to the agent or shell.

title: Detect prompt-injection and hostile-instruction content in repo and email inputs
description:
Context: Git metadata, source comments, patches, and mailing-list text may try to steer agents.
Scope: deterministic risk hints for normalized commit and email artifacts.
Acceptance criteria:
- Detect common prompt-injection phrases, credential/tool-execution requests, policy override language, and hidden-instruction markers in commit messages, diffs, path names, and email text.
- Add risk hints without deleting evidence needed for human review.
- Tests cover git commit bodies, source comments, patch text, and email bodies.
Risk notes:
- Prompt-injection detection routes safer handling; it must not be the only trust boundary.

title: Implement deterministic git range facts
description:
Context: Build the first evidence collector for local git repositories.
Scope: src/kernel_diffguard git collection modules, tests, CLI/API output schema.
Acceptance criteria:
- Given a local repo plus base X and target Y, emit stable JSON containing commits, authors, dates, touched paths, diff stats, renames, and signature/tag facts when present.
- Include synthetic-history fixtures covering merges, renames, empty commits, and unusual metadata.
- Tests run with pytest and outputs are deterministic.
Risk notes:
- Treat repository contents and metadata as hostile input; parse and reduce deterministically before any model-assisted analysis.
notes: Iteration notes: Implement single-commit parsing first, then deterministic commit-list/range iteration. Source-level analysis is the priority; binary/artifact checks are later.

title: Iterate a commit set or X..Y range deterministically
description:
Context: Range analysis composes single-commit artifacts into an ordered evidence set.
Scope: range iterator API/CLI, ordering rules, merge handling, output manifest.
Acceptance criteria:
- Given repo path plus base X and target Y, iterate commits in a deterministic order suitable for review.
- Support single commit, explicit list of commits, and X..Y range inputs.
- Emit a manifest containing range endpoints, traversal mode, commit count, skipped/unreachable errors, and per-commit artifact references.
- Include tests for linear history, merge commits, empty ranges, invalid revisions, and deterministic ordering.
Risk notes:
- Fail closed on ambiguous or unreachable revisions; never silently widen the range.

title: Run source-first static regression checks on commit artifacts
description:
Context: The first malicious-change checks should catch trivial source-level attacks before expensive analysis.
Scope: deterministic checks over commit/range facts plus optional local build/lint hooks.
Acceptance criteria:
- Flag removed or weakened tests, changes to CI/static-analysis configuration, generated-code churn, suspicious executable additions, and high-risk source paths.
- Provide hook points for compiler warnings or static analyzer delta checks when a build environment is available.
- Emit fact-backed findings with severity, evidence references, and uncertainty.
- Include tests for test deletion, warning-policy removal, and suspicious script/config changes.
Risk notes:
- Static rules are triage signals, not proof of maliciousness.

title: Design future system-profile impact analysis
description:
Context: Precise impact analysis eventually needs a target system profile, but the initial project should stay generally useful.
Scope: design only for kernel .config, hardware/module inventory, distro/vendor patches, runtime services, and threat model inputs.
Acceptance criteria:
- Document a profile schema for a single kernel config and optional hardware/runtime facts.
- Define how profile data would narrow retest recommendations without making generic analysis depend on it.
- Include examples for public Raspberry Pi style profiles.
Risk notes:
- Do not commit private/vendor target details or environment-specific facts to the public repo.

title: Generate release review packets
description:
Context: Combine facts and heuristics into a compact artifact for release/update review.
Scope: JSON and human-readable review packet formats.
Acceptance criteria:
- Packet includes facts, context links, risk hints, retest guidance, uncertainty, and suggested next checks.
- Every recommendation is traceable to evidence.
- Packet clearly separates deterministic facts from heuristics and model-assisted summaries.
Risk notes:
- Review packets support human decisions; they must not imply automatic trust decisions.

title: Define normalized evidence schemas and trust-boundary labels
description:
Context: Single-commit, commit-range, email-message, correlation, and finding outputs need compatible schemas before implementation hardens.
Scope: JSON schema/domain model for normalized artifacts, evidence references, truncation, risk hints, and trust-boundary labels.
Acceptance criteria:
- Define schemas for commit artifact, commit-range manifest, mailing-list message artifact, related-message candidate, and finding/recommendation.
- Include evidence-reference fields so every later heuristic or review-packet claim can point back to facts.
- Include hostile-input/truncation/risk-hint fields for git metadata, diffs, path names, and email text.
- Include tests or schema validation fixtures for representative artifacts.
Risk notes:
- Schemas are security boundaries; raw hostile fields must be visibly labeled and bounded.

title: Use Raspberry Pi kernel as the first public kernel exemplar
description:
Context: The public repo should demonstrate kernel-specific logic on a popular, active, fully public kernel tree rather than private/vendor context.
Scope: choose Raspberry Pi kernel source, document clone/update instructions, identify subsystem/list mapping implications.
Acceptance criteria:
- Select a current official/public Raspberry Pi kernel repository and branch strategy using latest official docs.
- Document why it is a good exemplar and where it differs from upstream Linux.
- Add fixture or smoke-test plan that avoids vendoring a large kernel tree into this repo.
Risk notes:
- Keep exemplar-specific assumptions separate from generic git/kernel logic.

title: Correlate patches with mailing-list discussion
description:
Context: Add public review context for reasoning about complex or suspicious changes.
Scope: Patch-id/subject matching, lore.kernel.org search, review tag extraction.
Acceptance criteria:
- Link commits or patch IDs to candidate mailing-list threads.
- Track vN patch series revisions where possible.
- Surface objections, acks, tested-by tags, and unresolved questions as evidence.
Risk notes:
- Mailing-list text is hostile input and must be reduced before higher-privilege or model-assisted processing.
notes: Iteration notes: Implement single-message parsing before related-message search. Candidate search should use timestamp windows, changed kernel area/subsystem, relevant list IDs, and patch/subject cues.

title: Research latest official Git, Python packaging, and Linux archive docs before locking interfaces
description:
Context: Tooling choices should use latest versions and official documentation rather than stale assumptions.
Scope: Git command plumbing/format docs, Python packaging/typing docs, lore.kernel.org/public-inbox docs, Linux kernel process docs.
Acceptance criteria:
- Capture links and decisions for current official docs used by parser/CLI/API design.
- Identify version-sensitive behavior and minimum supported tool versions.
- Recommend alternatives if a tool/API is stale or poorly documented.
Risk notes:
- This is a research bead; implementation beads should reference its findings when interfaces become sticky.

title: Assemble the source-first analysis pipeline skeleton
description:
Context: The project should compose parser, iterator, correlation, static-rule, prompt-injection, and later LLM stages without making any one stage monolithic.
Scope: CLI/API orchestration over normalized artifacts, no binary-analysis priority yet.
Acceptance criteria:
- Run a source-first pipeline over a single commit or X..Y range.
- Execute static rules and prompt-injection checks before any model-assisted analysis.
- Keep LLM/model analysis optional and fed only reduced/sanitized artifacts.
- Emit an intermediate artifact bundle suitable for review-packet generation.
Risk notes:
- Pipeline orchestration must not grant raw hostile inputs authority over tools, shell commands, secrets, or configuration.
